summary: Matched fighter function ft_80083E64 in src/melee/ft/ft_081B.c by replacing a compact wrapper around ft_800824A0 plus PAD_STACK(8) with an explicit static inline helper that reconstructs the collision-update sequence and uses an anonymous stack struct containing 8 bytes of padding before an ftCollisionBox. The public ft_80083E64 now only calls the callback when the inline helper returns true. The decomp.dev bot reported one new match: main/melee/ft/ft_081B ft_80083E64 moved from 99.90% to 100.00%, with matched code increasing by 292 bytes.
what_changed: Changed ft_80083E64 from directly computing bool do_cb = ft_800824A0(gobj, ecb), using PAD_STACK(8), and conditionally calling cb(gobj), into a wrapper around a new static inline bool ft_80083E64_inline(Fighter_GObj* gobj, ftCollisionBox* ecb).
The new inline helper declares an anonymous local stack struct with u8 pad[8] followed by ftCollisionBox collbox, replacing the prior PAD_STACK(8) approach.

The helper explicitly performs the collision-position update sequence: obtains Fighter* via GET_FIGHTER(gobj), uses CollData* coll = &fp->coll_data, assigns coll->last_pos = coll->cur_pos, assigns coll->cur_pos = fp->cur_pos, builds a facing-adjusted collision box via ft_80082838(&sp.collbox, ecb, fp->facing_dir), calls mpColl_8004730C(coll, box), writes fp->cur_pos = coll->cur_pos, then checks ft_80081A00(gobj).
The callback behavior remains at the ft_80083E64 boundary: cb(gobj) is called only if ft_80083E64_inline returns true.
The PR body says several approaches were tried, including pad stack macros, but the anonymous struct approach was the one that produced a 100% match.
smart_moves: Expanded the previously abstracted ft_800824A0 call into the exact operations needed for ft_80083E64, which gave the compiler more faithful local-variable and stack-layout behavior for this callsite.
Used a local anonymous struct to force 8 bytes of stack padding immediately before the ftCollisionBox local, preserving a specific stack arrangement that PAD_STACK(8) did not match here.
Kept the matched reconstruction isolated in a static inline helper, allowing ft_80083E64 itself to stay semantically simple as the callback gate while still producing the target code shape.
Stored the mpColl_8004730C result in a bool named temp_r31 and checked ft_80081A00 before returning it, reflecting the apparent register/lifetime constraint needed for matching.
decomp_lessons: When PAD_STACK macros do not yield the required stack layout, an anonymous local struct can be a more precise way to anchor padding and a typed local object together.

For small wrapper-like functions, matching may require inlining or locally re-expressing a callee's logic rather than relying on an existing helper call, even if the higher-level behavior is equivalent.

Collision code around Fighter::coll_data is sensitive to ordering: update last_pos, set cur_pos from fp->cur_pos, build the collision box using facing_dir, call mpColl_8004730C, then copy coll->cur_pos back to fp->cur_pos.
A static inline helper can be a practical tactic for shaping generated code while preserving the exported function signature and callback-facing control flow.
The evidence for intent is mostly from the PR body and diff; there were no human review comments explaining broader style preferences.
naming_conventions: New helper followed the local-address function naming style with suffix: ft_80083E64_inline.
Temporary register-sensitive boolean was named temp_r31, matching a common decomp convention for values believed to live in a specific register.
Local pointers used short subsystem names already common in the fighter code: fp for Fighter*, coll for CollData*, box for ftCollisionBox*.
The local anonymous stack-struct instance was named sp, making its role as stack storage explicit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace PAD_STACK(8) with an anonymous struct containing u8 pad[8] and ftCollisionBox collbox to control local stack layout.
Use static inline to encourage the compiler to emit the helper body in the desired context while leaving ft_80083E64 as a small callback wrapper.
Preserve value lifetime by assigning mpColl_8004730C(coll, box) into temp_r31 before calling ft_80081A00(gobj), then returning false if ft_80081A00 succeeds and true only if temp_r31 is true.
Use &sp.collbox as the destination object passed to ft_80082838 so the stack-local ftCollisionBox is materialized in the expected location.
Decomp.dev reported the tactic achieved a new 100.00% match for ft_80083E64 in main/melee/ft/ft_081B.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the PR slice.
The PR author noted this was generated/tested while trying Codex with GPT 5.4 and said the anonymous struct approach was the only attempted approach that produced a 100% match.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 55.47% overall, +292 bytes, with ft_80083E64 moving from 99.90% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other fighter collision functions using PAD_STACK near ftCollisionBox locals that may need anonymous struct stack layout.
Search for ft_800824A0 callsites that are nearly matching and may require explicit inlined collision-position logic.
Compare ft_80083E64_inline against neighboring ft_081B.c functions for reusable CollData update patterns.
Look for temp_r31 boolean lifetimes around collision callbacks where a call to ft_80081A00 occurs before callback dispatch.
